title: Reapply SBOM and release evidence improvements

sources.reason: Prior implementation contains the intended SBOM identity and release evidence changes, but is based on stale main and must be selectively reimplemented.
Current supply-chain evidence generation and validation boundary.
Current public release evidence and pre-publication validation boundary.
Current documented release and evidence contract.
Existing regression coverage for SBOM and provenance behavior.

riskAssessment.reason: The change affects generated release evidence and public validation behavior; stale or mismatched identities must fail closed.

executionDecision.reason: The intended scope and acceptance are explicit; implementation will preserve current main behavior outside supply-chain evidence.

preReviewWarnings: Do not cherry-pick the stale commit wholesale; preserve current main release and governance behavior.
Generated SBOM, provenance, and release digest files must be regenerated by command.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record scope and final evidence checkpoints for a medium-risk release evidence change.

acceptance: SBOM generation uses the current CycloneDX API and records the application identity, stable component references, dependency relationships, and tool metadata without reverting current main changes.

Release evidence validation reports drift when any of the five release evidence files differs from release.json, and vulnerability mapping rejects a pip-audit finding that has no matching SBOM component.

The current .github/workflows/release.yml, .github/workflows/smoke.yml, and .github/workflows/compatibility.yml contents remain unchanged, while focused regression tests pass for the reimplemented supply-chain behavior.
The repository supply-chain commands regenerate SBOM/provenance/release-digests, the focused and full project tests pass, and the complete AI Cockpit finish checks archive this Work Item.
guidelines: Do not hand-edit generated SBOM, provenance, or release digest artifacts.
Preserve the current main release workflow and governance archive lifecycle.
Keep supply-chain evidence identity explicit and fail closed when mappings are incomplete.

intent.problem: The prior SBOM and release-evidence improvement was stranded on a stale local branch and was not included in the v0.5.26 release.
constraints: Start from the synchronized latest main.
Reimplement only the supply-chain evidence improvements, without rolling back current governance or release changes.
Keep generated artifacts command-owned and preserve the immutable v0.5.26 release.
intent.rationale: A focused reimplementation avoids importing stale branch history while retaining the security and evidence improvements that motivated ab850da.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: SBOM identity and dependency metadata are stable and verifiable
release evidence detects digest drift and preserves explicit source identity
current main release and governance workflows remain unchanged outside declared scope

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized Task 1 implementation and command-owned regeneration of SBOM, provenance, and release-digest artifacts.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
